tasks.description: task_id -> task state record. Each record's `status` is one of: queued, running, succeeded, failed, timed_out, cancelled, schema_violation, skipped, gate_failed, skipped_by_route, expanded. `expanded` (foreach parents, F6) is deliberately non-terminal; succeeded and skipped_by_route are the terminal statuses that satisfy dependencies without failing the run. Documentation-level: this schema is not enforced at runtime (validateRunState checks the top-level shape only).

expanded_tasks.description: Foreach child task definitions appended at expansion time (F6, spec §6.2): `<parent>.<index>` ids derived from the parent definition plus one resolved item each. Every entry has a matching state.tasks record; resume rebuilds missing entries by replaying tasks_expanded events.
